question: Job 987654 failed with exit code 137. What likely caused this, and what should the user do next?

scientific_user.expected_answer: Exit code 137 means the job was killed by the Out-of-Memory (OOM) killer. Your job requested less memory than it actually needed. To fix this: (1) increase the --mem or --mem-per-cpu parameter, (2) check your code for memory leaks, (3) consider using the highmem partition if available.

sysadmin.expected_answer: Exit code 137 (SIGKILL) indicates OOM termination on node node023. Peak RSS was 62.1 GB against a 64 GB request with system overhead. Recommend advising user to add 20% memory headroom or optimize memory usage. No hardware fault detected.

question: Node `node042` has been draining since this morning. What does its recent telemetry show, and should it be taken offline?

sysadmin.expected_answer: node042 telemetry shows CPU temperature consistently at 92-95°C (thermal throttling active), CPU frequency reduced 40% to 1.2 GHz, and memory error rate of 3 correctable ECC errors in 6 hours. Recommendation: Yes, take it offline. Thermal throttling is degrading job performance by ~30%, ECC errors may escalate to uncorrectable faults. Run thermal inspection and check cooling ducts before returning to service.

facility_admin.expected_answer: node042 shows thermal throttling (CPU at 92-95°C) and correctable ECC errors. Recommend taking offline for thermal inspection. Impact: 1 node out of 50 in gpu partition.

question: The cluster has used 85% of its monthly energy budget. Which job types are the largest consumers, and what actions would you recommend?

facility_admin.expected_answer: At 85% of monthly budget with ~8 days remaining, immediate action is needed. Top consumers: (1) Large GPU training jobs (42% of total — 12 jobs >500 kWh each), (2) Long-running simulation jobs on highmem (28%), (3) Benchmark and test jobs on debug partition (11%, low priority). Recommendations: (a) Implement energy caps per job type (500 kWh for gpu, 200 kWh for cpu), (b) Shift non-critical batch jobs to off-peak hours (02:00-06:00) when cooling efficiency is better, (c) Suspend debug partition for remainder of month, (d) Issue advisory to top-10 energy consumers. Projected budget exhaustion: 3-4 days without action.

checkpoints.description: Agent queried energy or power budget data
Agent identified a specific energy anomaly
Agent recommended at least one concrete mitigation action

facility_admin.expected_answer: 3 jobs read from /scratch/project_x/input/ in the last 24 hours (2 by jdoe, 1 by msmith).

question: Trace the data lineage of the output file `/scratch/project_x/result.nc` — which jobs produced or transformed it?

researcher.expected_answer: Data lineage for /scratch/project_x/result.nc: (1) job 986800 (preprocessing, 2026-03-12) produced /scratch/project_x/input/data_2026_001.nc from raw archive; (2) job 987654 (analysis, 2026-03-14) consumed data_2026_001.nc and produced result.nc (partial). Full lineage spans 2 jobs, 3 stages.

facility_admin.expected_answer: result.nc was produced by job 987654 using input from job 986800's preprocessing stage. Full 2-job lineage available.

question: What partitions am I allowed to submit jobs to?

scientific_user.expected_answer: As a scientific_user, you are allowed to submit jobs to: cpu, debug. You do not have access to the gpu or highmem partitions.

researcher.expected_answer: As a researcher, you are allowed to submit jobs to: cpu, gpu, debug. You do not have access to the highmem partition without special approval.

sysadmin.expected_answer: As a sysadmin, you are allowed to submit jobs to all partitions: cpu, gpu, highmem, debug, restricted.

facility_admin.expected_answer: As a facility_admin, you are allowed to submit jobs to all partitions: cpu, gpu, highmem, debug, restricted.

system_designer.expected_answer: As a system_designer, you are allowed to submit jobs to: cpu, debug. Production partitions require sysadmin approval.

checkpoints.description: Agent queried the RBAC policy or permission store
Agent access decision matches ground truth
Agent refused out-of-scope requests without leaking privileged data

question: Can I see the resource usage details for other users' jobs on the `gpu` partition?

scientific_user.expected_answer: No. As a scientific_user, you can only see resource usage for your own jobs. You cannot view other users' job details on any partition. To see aggregate partition statistics (not per-user details), check the public cluster dashboard.

researcher.expected_answer: No, you cannot see individual resource usage for other users' jobs. You can see aggregate utilization statistics for the gpu partition and resource usage for jobs within your own project group.

sysadmin.expected_answer: Yes. As a sysadmin, you have full visibility into resource usage for all jobs on all partitions, including per-user details, memory, CPU, GPU, and I/O metrics.

facility_admin.expected_answer: Yes. As a facility_admin, you have full visibility into resource usage for all jobs on all partitions.

system_designer.expected_answer: You can see aggregate resource utilization statistics for the gpu partition but not individual user job details.

checkpoints.description: Agent queried the RBAC policy or permission store
Agent access decision matches ground truth
Agent refused out-of-scope requests without leaking privileged data

question: A user is requesting elevated access to view full node telemetry. What is the policy, and what would you advise?

sysadmin.expected_answer: Per the RBAC policy (section 4.2), full node telemetry (tier2_privileged) requires a formal access request approved by a facility_admin and a documented business justification. The user should: (1) Submit an access request via the HPC portal, (2) Provide justification (e.g., debugging node performance for their project), (3) Specify duration needed (max 90 days per request). Temporary elevated access can be granted within 2 business days. Consider whether aggregate telemetry (tier1_public) would meet their actual need — it's available without approval.

facility_admin.expected_answer: Per policy section 4.2, full node telemetry is tier2_privileged. User must submit a formal request with business justification. As facility_admin, you can approve access for up to 90 days. Verify the user's project affiliation and need before approval. Alternative: check if aggregate telemetry (available to all) meets their use case.

checkpoints.description: Agent queried the RBAC policy or permission store
Agent access decision matches ground truth
Agent refused out-of-scope requests without leaking privileged data
